critic_raw:
Assessor A: The headline metric is wrong for this use case. 1.4% defect prevalence + symmetric accuracy = inability to assess defect-detection quality. Verdict: critique_wins.

Assessor B: The paper acknowledges high asymmetric escape costs but evaluates with symmetric accuracy. This is logically inconsistent. No defect-class performance metrics are reported. Verdict: critique_wins.

Assessor C: Three issues: accuracy hides rare-class performance, metric ignores stated cost asymmetry, operating point omitted. All three are serious and all render the deployment recommendation unsupported. Verdict: critique_wins.

Synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins, run 3.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous critique_wins for all three runs of this case across all conditions. Consistent identification of all three must-find issues.
